---
title: "Runtime, Memory, and Execution"
---

# Runtime, Memory, and Execution

Range: `0609-0684`.

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                               | Summary                                                                                                                                    |
| ----- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0609  | [address space](#rune-0609)        | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0610  | [aliasing](#rune-0610)             | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0611  | [allocation](#rune-0611)           | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0612  | [allocator](#rune-0612)            | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0613  | [arena](#rune-0613)                | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0614  | [borrow {Run}](#rune-0614)         | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0615  | [cache {Run}](#rune-0615)          | Keep likely things close so future cost falls. Shadow: stale truth delivered at machine speed.                                             |
| 0616  | [cache line {Run}](#rune-0616)     | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0617  | [call frame](#rune-0617)           | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0618  | [call stack](#rune-0618)           | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0619  | [closure environment](#rune-0619)  | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0620  | [compaction {Run}](#rune-0620)     | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0621  | [copy-on-write {Run}](#rune-0621)  | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0622  | [core dump {Run}](#rune-0622)      | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0623  | [deallocation](#rune-0623)         | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0624  | [destructor {Run}](#rune-0624)     | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0625  | [dynamic allocation](#rune-0625)   | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0626  | [eager evaluation](#rune-0626)     | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0627  | [escape analysis](#rune-0627)      | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0628  | [execution context](#rune-0628)    | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0629  | [finalizer](#rune-0629)            | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0630  | [frame pointer](#rune-0630)        | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0631  | [garbage collection](#rune-0631)   | A reclamation word; unreachable memory is gathered and returned.                                                                           |
| 0632  | [generation](#rune-0632)           | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0633  | [handle {Run}](#rune-0633)         | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0634  | [heap](#rune-0634)                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0635  | [heap object](#rune-0635)          | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0636  | [hot path {Run}](#rune-0636)       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0637  | [inline cache](#rune-0637)         | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0638  | [interpreter loop](#rune-0638)     | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0639  | [JIT](#rune-0639)                  | A just-in-time word; translation happens at runtime rather than earlier in the forge.                                                      |
| 0640  | [layout {Run}](#rune-0640)         | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0641  | [lazy evaluation](#rune-0641)      | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0642  | [leak {Run}](#rune-0642)           | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0643  | [lifetime {Run}](#rune-0643)       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0644  | [live set](#rune-0644)             | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0645  | [locality](#rune-0645)             | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0646  | [lock-free {Run}](#rune-0646)      | A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.                                                                          |
| 0647  | [mark phase](#rune-0647)           | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0648  | [memory {Run}](#rune-0648)         | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0649  | [memory barrier](#rune-0649)       | An ordering word; it forbids certain reorderings across threads or hardware boundaries.                                                    |
| 0650  | [memory map](#rune-0650)           | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0651  | [memory pool](#rune-0651)          | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0652  | [memory safety](#rune-0652)        | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0653  | [move semantics](#rune-0653)       | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0654  | [object header](#rune-0654)        | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0655  | [page {Run}](#rune-0655)           | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0656  | [page fault](#rune-0656)           | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0657  | [pinning](#rune-0657)              | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0658  | [pointer {Run}](#rune-0658)        | A location word; it does not hold the thing, but where the thing may be found.                                                             |
| 0659  | [pool allocator](#rune-0659)       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0660  | [prefetch](#rune-0660)             | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0661  | [quiescence](#rune-0661)           | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0662  | [reclaim](#rune-0662)              | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0663  | [reference count](#rune-0663)      | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0664  | [register allocation](#rune-0664)  | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0665  | [root set](#rune-0665)             | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0666  | [runtime](#rune-0666)              | A living word; the conditions under which the program presently exists and executes.                                                       |
| 0667  | [sandbox {Run}](#rune-0667)        | A confinement word; actions are permitted only within a restricted space.                                                                  |
| 0668  | [shadow stack](#rune-0668)         | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0669  | [slab allocator](#rune-0669)       | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0670  | [stack {Run}](#rune-0670)          | A layering word; order matters and the most recent thing is nearest the top.                                                               |
| 0671  | [stack frame](#rune-0671)          | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0672  | [stop-the-world](#rune-0672)       | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0673  | [sweep phase](#rune-0673)          | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0674  | [tail call](#rune-0674)            | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0675  | [thread-local storage](#rune-0675) | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0676  | [TLB](#rune-0676)                  | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0677  | [tracing collector](#rune-0677)    | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0678  | [unbox](#rune-0678)                | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0679  | [user heap](#rune-0679)            | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                             |
| 0680  | [virtual memory](#rune-0680)       | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0681  | [VM](#rune-0681)                   | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                    |
| 0682  | [wait-free {Run}](#rune-0682)      | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                         |
| 0683  | [write barrier](#rune-0683)        | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                 |
| 0684  | [zero-copy {Run}](#rune-0684)      | Data movement stripped down to the minimum number of duplications. Use it when the bottleneck is physical movement rather than arithmetic. |

## Entries

### [0609] address space {#rune-0609}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0610] aliasing {#rune-0610}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0611] allocation {#rune-0611}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Pocket gloss:** Reserve memory for something that is about to live.

### [0612] allocator {#rune-0612}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0613] arena {#rune-0613}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0614] borrow {Run} {#rune-0614}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0615] cache {Run} {#rune-0615}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Keep likely things close so future cost falls.

**Shadow:** stale truth delivered at machine speed.

**Pocket gloss:** Keep likely things close so repeated access costs less.

### [0616] cache line {Run} {#rune-0616}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0617] call frame {#rune-0617}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0618] call stack {#rune-0618}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0619] closure environment {#rune-0619}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0620] compaction {Run} {#rune-0620}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0621] copy-on-write {Run} {#rune-0621}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Pocket gloss:** Share until mutation forces a real duplicate.

### [0622] core dump {Run} {#rune-0622}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0623] deallocation {#rune-0623}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0624] destructor {Run} {#rune-0624}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0625] dynamic allocation {#rune-0625}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0626] eager evaluation {#rune-0626}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0627] escape analysis {#rune-0627}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0628] execution context {#rune-0628}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0629] finalizer {#rune-0629}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0630] frame pointer {#rune-0630}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0631] garbage collection {#rune-0631}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A reclamation word; unreachable memory is gathered and returned.

**Pocket gloss:** Automatic reclamation of memory no longer reachable.

### [0632] generation {#rune-0632}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0633] handle {Run} {#rune-0633}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0634] heap {#rune-0634}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

**Pocket gloss:** Region for dynamically allocated objects.

### [0635] heap object {#rune-0635}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0636] hot path {Run} {#rune-0636}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Pocket gloss:** The path where small inefficiencies burn real time.

### [0637] inline cache {#rune-0637}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0638] interpreter loop {#rune-0638}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0639] JIT {#rune-0639}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A just-in-time word; translation happens at runtime rather than earlier in the forge.

### [0640] layout {Run} {#rune-0640}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0641] lazy evaluation {#rune-0641}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0642] leak {Run} {#rune-0642}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0643] lifetime {Run} {#rune-0643}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Pocket gloss:** How long a value may legally remain alive.

### [0644] live set {#rune-0644}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0645] locality {#rune-0645}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Pocket gloss:** The performance dividend from keeping related data physically close.

### [0646] lock-free {Run} {#rune-0646}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.

### [0647] mark phase {#rune-0647}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0648] memory {Run} {#rune-0648}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0649] memory barrier {#rune-0649}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An ordering word; it forbids certain reorderings across threads or hardware boundaries.

**Pocket gloss:** An ordering fence for memory effects across threads or cores.

### [0650] memory map {#rune-0650}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0651] memory pool {#rune-0651}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0652] memory safety {#rune-0652}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

**Pocket gloss:** Protection against illegal memory access and corruption.

### [0653] move semantics {#rune-0653}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0654] object header {#rune-0654}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0655] page {Run} {#rune-0655}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0656] page fault {#rune-0656}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0657] pinning {#rune-0657}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

**Pocket gloss:** Keep an object fixed in place so other machinery can safely refer to it.

### [0658] pointer {Run} {#rune-0658}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A location word; it does not hold the thing, but where the thing may be found.

### [0659] pool allocator {#rune-0659}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0660] prefetch {#rune-0660}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0661] quiescence {#rune-0661}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0662] reclaim {#rune-0662}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0663] reference count {#rune-0663}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

**Pocket gloss:** Ownership tracked by counting how many references remain.

### [0664] register allocation {#rune-0664}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0665] root set {#rune-0665}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0666] runtime {#rune-0666}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living word; the conditions under which the program presently exists and executes.

### [0667] sandbox {Run} {#rune-0667}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A confinement word; actions are permitted only within a restricted space.

### [0668] shadow stack {#rune-0668}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0669] slab allocator {#rune-0669}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0670] stack {Run} {#rune-0670}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A layering word; order matters and the most recent thing is nearest the top.

**Pocket gloss:** Region for call frames and short-lived local state.

### [0671] stack frame {#rune-0671}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0672] stop-the-world {#rune-0672}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0673] sweep phase {#rune-0673}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0674] tail call {#rune-0674}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0675] thread-local storage {#rune-0675}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0676] TLB {#rune-0676}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0677] tracing collector {#rune-0677}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0678] unbox {#rune-0678}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0679] user heap {#rune-0679}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.

### [0680] virtual memory {#rune-0680}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0681] VM {#rune-0681}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.

### [0682] wait-free {Run} {#rune-0682}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.

### [0683] write barrier {#rune-0683}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.

### [0684] zero-copy {Run} {#rune-0684}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Data movement stripped down to the minimum number of duplications. Use it when the bottleneck is physical movement rather than arithmetic.

**Pocket gloss:** Move data without making redundant copies.
